text: Last class you wrote: I am writing to confirm our meeting.
Now we ask: On a call, how do you report what the other person said?

Hello Doctor Nilo. Can you hear me? Is your connection good today?
Yes, I can hear you well. There is no background noise on my side.
Perfect. I am the host, so I will let the others in from the waiting room.
Thank you. If my connection drops, I will call back or join by phone.
Good idea. After the meeting, I will send the minutes to everyone.
Excellent. I will follow up with my questions by email. Let us wrap up on time.
Can you hear me? My connection is good.
Please mute your microphone when you are not speaking.
If we get disconnected, I will call you back.
She said that she would send the minutes after the call.
Let us wrap up the call and follow up by email.

Hello Doctor Nilo, this is Sofia from the FIFA Leadership in Football program office. I am leaving you a quick voicemail about our video call next week. First, the call is going to start at three in the afternoon, your time in Sao Paulo, so please connect a few minutes early. Second, we will use the same video platform as last time, and I will be the host, so I will let you in from the waiting room. If your connection is weak, do not worry, you can join by phone and dial the number in the invitation. One more thing: please keep your microphone on mute when you are not speaking, because the background noise from many participants can be a problem. After the call, I will send you the minutes by email, so you do not need to take notes. If you cannot reach me before the meeting, just send a short message and I will call you back. Thank you, Doctor, and I look forward to speaking with you.

Let me tell you about the video call I had yesterday with the FIFA program office. Sofia called me in the morning, and she said that the main call would start at three in the afternoon. She told me that she would be the host, and she explained that we would use the same platform as before. She also said that the connection last time was a little weak, so she asked me to join a few minutes early. During the call, my colleague from Argentina said that his microphone was not working, so he joined by phone instead. The coordinator told us that she would send the minutes by email after the meeting, and she asked everyone to follow up with their questions in writing. At the end, she said that she was very happy with our progress, and she promised that she would call me back next week to confirm the next steps. It was a long call, but I understood almost everything, and I felt much more confident in English.

Here are the steps of the call. First, the host opens the meeting and lets everyone in. Then we check the connection and mute our microphones. After that, the host shares the main points and we discuss them. Next, the host takes the minutes. Finally, we wrap up and follow up by email.
She said that she would send the minutes after the call.
